task: Workbench (Enfusion Workbench) is running. 1. Bring it to focus (click on its window or taskbar icon). 2. Look for the Editors menu. 3. Click Editors > World Editor to open the world editor. 4. Wait 10 seconds. 5. Then click Plugins > ELOS > AI Generate Mission. 6. Wait 5 seconds. 7. Screenshot + report result.

text: I'll help you with that. Let me start by taking a screenshot to see the current state of the screen.

I can see the Enfusion Workbench Launcher is open, but I need to find the actual Workbench window. I can see there's an "ELOS - Live Mission Editor" window in the top right. Let me look for the Workbench in the taskbar. Let me check the taskbar for the Workbench application.

I can see the "Enfusion Workbench Launcher" is visible. I need to look for the actual Workbench application in the taskbar. Let me look more carefully at the taskbar at the bottom of the screen to find the Workbench icon.

I can see the "Enfusion Workbench Launcher" window in the middle of the screen. The actual Workbench (with the Editors menu) might be hidden behind other windows. Let me look at the taskbar more carefully. I'll scroll through the taskbar or look for the Workbench application. Let me check the system tray and taskbar icons at the bottom.

The taskbar seems to be showing various icons. Let me try clicking on the Workbench Launcher first, and then look for the main Workbench window. Actually, I notice the "Enfusion Workbench Launcher" is currently showing a "Projects" dialog. The actual main Workbench window might be behind other windows. Let me try clicking on the taskbar area to find the Workbench.

I can see a Discord overlay notification appeared. I can see the Enfusion Workbench Launcher is showing. The actual Workbench main window might be minimized or hidden. Let me dismiss the Discord notification first and look for the Workbench in the taskbar. Let me click somewhere else to dismiss the Discord popup.

I can see there's a new popup window that appeared on the right side. It seems to have some content with a "Continue" button. But more importantly, I need to find the actual Enfusion Workbench main window. Let me look at the taskbar to find the Workbench. Let me right-click on the taskbar to see all running windows, or use Alt+Tab to see all open windows.
I can see a Windows PowerShell window that switched to focus. I need to find the Enfusion Workbench. Let me use Alt+Tab again to cycle through the open windows to find the Workbench.

I need to find the Enfusion Workbench. Let me look at the taskbar at the bottom of the screen more carefully. I can see various application icons. Let me try using the Windows Task View or look at the taskbar to identify the Workbench. Let me try pressing Win+Tab to see all open windows.

Now I can see all open windows in the Task View. I can see:
1. ELOS - Live Mis... (top left)
2. Various PowerShell windows
3. Claude window
4. Discord
5. elos-reload.ahk (AutoHotkey script)
6. Steam, Downloads, etc.

I don't see the main Enfusion Workbench window directly. The "ELOS - Live Mis..." at the top left is the ELOS Live Mission Editor I saw earlier. Let me look more carefully - I notice the "elos-reload.ahk" windows show an AutoHotkey script with a warning about `A_UserProfile` and a file path containing "ArmaReforgerWorkbench\profile\ELOS.spec.js". This might be related.

But I need to find the actual Workbench application. Let me press Escape and look at the taskbar more carefully, or check if Workbench is running but minimized/hidden.

I can see there's a partially visible window on the right side with what appears to be a "Continue" button and a path showing "OS\spec.js". This could be related to the AutoHotkey popup. Let me look at the taskbar more carefully. I can see there are some taskbar icons at the bottom. Let me look at the system tray and the taskbar to find the Workbench.

Let me look at the area in the middle-right of the screen - I can see there's a window partially visible. Also I notice the text in one of the PowerShell windows says "ArmaReforgerWorkb..." and ".gproj" which suggests the Workbench might be running. 

Let me check the taskbar icons more carefully by looking at the bottom of the screen. I'll try right-clicking on the taskbar to see what's running.

I can now see the "elos-reload.ahk" popup clearly. It shows a warning about `A_UserProfile` variable and mentions the path `\Documents\my games\ArmaReforgerWorkbench\profile\ELOS\spec.js`. There's a "Continue" button. I need to click "Continue" to dismiss this dialog, and then look for the Workbench. Let me click "Continue".

Now it shows an "Error: This global variable has not been assigned a value. Specifically: A_UserProfile". This is the AutoHotkey script `elos-reload.ahk` that has an error. Let me click "Continue" again to dismiss this error and proceed.
